  - id: 01-02
    name: 'List policies endpoint with status filtering'
    description: >
      Implement listPolicies query returning workspace policies with title, status, version,
      rules_count, created_at. Support optional ?status= query parameter filter. Wire to
      GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies.
    criteria:
      - 'GET /policies returns all workspace policies with title, status, version, rules_count, created_at'
      - 'GET /policies?status=active returns only policies matching that status'
      - 'Empty workspace returns { policies: [] }'
      - 'Filter with no matches returns empty array'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-1: admin sees all policies (1.7)'
      - 'milestone-1: status filter returns matching (1.8)'
      - 'milestone-1: empty workspace returns empty list (1.9)'
      - 'milestone-1: filter no matches returns empty (1.10)'
  - id: 01-03
    name: 'Policy request validation'
    description: >
      Implement pure validation functions for policy creation bodies: required non-empty title,
      at least one rule, valid predicate structure (field/operator/value), valid effect
      (allow/deny only). Return 400 with descriptive error on failure.
    criteria:
      - 'Missing or empty title returns 400 with error containing "title"'
      - 'Empty rules array returns 400 with error containing "rule"'
      - 'Invalid predicate structure returns 400'
      - 'Invalid effect value returns 400'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-2: missing title rejected (2.4)'
      - 'milestone-2: missing rules rejected (2.5)'
      - 'milestone-2: invalid predicate rejected (2.6)'
      - 'milestone-2: invalid effect rejected (2.7)'
- id: '02'
  name: 'Policy creation endpoint'
  steps:
  - id: 02-01
    name: 'Create draft policy via API'
    description: >
      Implement POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies. Persist policy with draft status,
      version 1, workspace scope. Support title, description, selector, rules,
      human_veto_required, max_ttl fields. Return 201 with { policy_id }.
    criteria:
      - 'POST returns 201 with policy_id for valid payload'
      - 'Created policy has status=draft, version=1'
      - 'All fields (title, description, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl) persist correctly'
      - 'Multiple rules at different priorities are preserved'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-2: admin creates draft with deny rule (2.1)'
      - 'milestone-2: admin creates with multiple rules (2.2)'
      - 'milestone-2: admin creates with selector (2.3)'
  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Activate policy with governance edges'
    description: >
      Implement PATCH /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/activate. Validate draft status
      prerequisite (409 for active/deprecated/superseded). Transition to active, create
      governing and protects relation edges. Return 200 with { status: "active" }.
    criteria:
      - 'Draft policy transitions to active with governing and protects edges created'
      - 'Already-active policy returns 409'
      - 'Deprecated policy returns 409'
      - 'Superseded policy returns 409'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-3: admin activates draft (3.1)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate active (3.2)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate deprecated (3.3)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate superseded (3.4)'
  - id: 02-03
    name: 'Deprecate policy with edge removal'
    description: >
      Implement PATCH /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/deprecate. Validate active status
      prerequisite (409 for draft/deprecated). Transition to deprecated, remove governing
      and protects edges. Policy excluded from loadActivePolicies results.
    criteria:
      - 'Active policy transitions to deprecated with edges removed'
      - 'Deprecated policy excluded from active policy loading'
      - 'Draft policy returns 409'
      - 'Already-deprecated policy returns 409'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-3: admin deprecates active (3.5)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot deprecate draft (3.6)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot deprecate deprecated (3.7)'
- id: '03'
  name: 'Policy detail + version creation + policy trace'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'Policy detail endpoint with edges'
    description: >
      Implement GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id. Return full PolicyRecord with
      resolved governing/protects edges via getPolicyEdges query. Return 404 for
      non-existent policies. Include edge state appropriate to policy status.
    criteria:
      - 'Detail returns full record: id, title, description, version, status, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl, created_at'
      - 'Active policy detail includes populated governing and protects edges'
      - 'Deprecated policy detail shows empty edges'
      - 'Non-existent policy returns 404'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-4: admin views full draft detail (4.1)'
      - 'milestone-4: active policy includes edges (4.2)'
      - 'milestone-4: deprecated policy shows empty edges (4.3)'
      - 'milestone-4: non-existent returns 404 (4.4)'
  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Version creation with supersede chain'
    description: >
      Implement POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/versions. Only active policies
      can spawn new versions (409 for draft/deprecated). Create new policy record with
      incremented version, draft status, supersedes reference to parent, and copied fields.
      Return 201 with { policy_id, version }.
    criteria:
      - 'New version created as draft with incremented version number and supersedes reference'
      - 'All fields copied from parent (title, description, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl)'
      - 'Cannot create version from draft policy (409)'
      - 'Cannot create version from deprecated policy (409)'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-5: admin creates version from active (5.1)'
      - 'milestone-5: cannot version draft (5.2)'
      - 'milestone-5: cannot version deprecated (5.3)'
  - id: 03-03
    name: 'Supersede atomicity + version history + policy trace persistence'
    description: >
      Extend activate endpoint: when activating a policy with supersedes reference,
      atomically transition old version to superseded and remove its edges. Implement
      GET /policies/:id/versions returning ordered version chain via recursive supersedes
      traversal. Ensure policy_trace field on intent evaluation persists trace entries
      and links back to policy detail.
    criteria:
      - 'Activating new version atomically supersedes old version and moves edges'
      - 'GET /versions returns all versions ordered by version number'
      - 'Version history for non-existent policy returns 404'
      - 'Policy trace entries on intent evaluation link to retrievable policy details'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-5: supersede atomicity (5.4)'
      - 'milestone-5: version history returns chain (5.5)'
      - 'milestone-5: version history 404 (5.6)'
      - 'milestone-4: version chain in detail (4.5)'
      - 'milestone-4: single version chain (4.6)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace with matching rules (6.1)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace matched and unmatched (6.2)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace links to policy detail (6.3)'
      - 'milestone-6: empty trace no active policies (6.4)'
- id: '04'
  name: 'React UI — policy list + create dialog + detail page'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'Policy list page and sidebar navigation'
    description: >
      Add Policies entry to WorkspaceSidebar. Create PoliciesPage with TanStack Router
      route. Fetch and display policies in a table with title, status badge, version,
      rules count, created date. Include status filter dropdown.
    criteria:
      - 'Policies link visible in workspace sidebar navigation'
      - 'Policy list page displays all workspace policies in tabular format'
      - 'Status filter dropdown filters the displayed list'
      - 'Empty state shown when no policies exist'
  - id: 04-02
    name: 'Create policy dialog with rule builder'
    description: >
      Create dialog for new policy creation. Form fields: title (required), description,
      selector (agent_role), human_veto_required toggle, max_ttl. Inline rule builder
      for adding rules with condition (field/operator/value), effect (allow/deny), and
      priority. Submit calls POST /policies endpoint.
    criteria:
      - 'Dialog opens from list page action button'
      - 'Form validates required title and at least one rule before submission'
      - 'Rules can be added and removed inline'
      - 'Successful creation navigates to policy detail page'
  - id: 04-03
    name: 'Policy detail page with lifecycle actions'
    description: >
      Create PolicyDetailPage showing full policy record, governance edges, and version
      chain. Include activate/deprecate action buttons with confirmation. Show version
      history list linking to each version. Create-new-version button for active policies.
    criteria:
      - 'Detail page displays all policy fields, rules, and governance edges'
      - 'Activate button shown for draft policies, deprecate for active'
      - 'Version history list shows all versions with status indicators'
      - 'Create new version button appears for active policies'
- id: '05'
  name: 'Advanced UI — rule builder + policy trace + version diff'
  steps:
  - id: 05-01
    name: 'Rule builder with field autocomplete'
    description: >
      Enhance rule builder with autocomplete for condition field names sourced from
      known IntentEvaluationContext fields (action_spec.action, action_spec.provider,
      budget_limit.amount, etc.). Operator dropdown adapts to field type. Value input
      with type-appropriate controls.
    criteria:
      - 'Field input offers autocomplete suggestions from IntentEvaluationContext'
      - 'Operator dropdown shows relevant operators for selected field type'
      - 'Composed rule preview shows human-readable condition summary'
  - id: 05-02
    name: 'Policy trace view and version diff'
    description: >
      Add policy trace section to intent review/consent page showing which policies and
      rules matched during evaluation. Each trace entry links to policy detail. Implement
      version diff view comparing two version records side-by-side with structural diff
      of rules, selector, and metadata computed client-side.
    criteria:
      - 'Intent review page shows policy trace with matched/unmatched rule indicators'
      - 'Trace entries link to corresponding policy detail pages'
      - 'Version diff highlights added, removed, and changed rules between two versions'
      - 'Diff view accessible from version history list'
implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
  - app/src/server/policy/
  - app/src/client/components/policy/
  - app/src/client/pages/
  test_directories:
  - tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/
  excluded_patterns:
  - node_modules/**
  - dist/**
validation:
  status: pending
  reviewer: atlas
  approved_at: null

file.content:
---
description: "Dispatches an expert reviewer agent to critique workflow artifacts. Use when a roadmap, implementation, or step needs quality review before proceeding."
argument-hint: '[agent] [artifact-type] [artifact-path] - Example: @software-crafter task "roadmap.yaml"'
---

# NW-REVIEW: Expert Critique and Quality Assurance

**Wave**: CROSS_WAVE
**Agent**: Dynamic (nw-*-reviewer)

## Overview

Dispatches expert reviewer agent to critique workflow artifacts. Takes base agent name, appends `-reviewer`, invokes with artifact. Reviewer agent owns all review methodology|criteria|output format.

## Review Philosophy: Radical Candor

Every review MUST embody Radical Candor — kind AND clear, specific AND sincere:

- **Care personally**: Acknowledge what works. Understand author's intent before critiquing. Include at least one genuine `praise:` per review.
- **Challenge directly**: Be specific about what is wrong and WHY. Ground feedback in evidence|consequences, not preference. Never soften security/data-loss issues.
- **Avoid ruinous empathy**: Never "LGTM" when real issues exist. Hedging ("maybe consider possibly...") on blocking concerns is a review failure.
- **Avoid obnoxious aggression**: Never "this is terrible" without constructive alternative. Focus on work, not author. Explain "why" behind every critique.

## Feedback Format: Conventional Comments

All findings MUST use Conventional Comments labels:

| Label | Purpose | Blocking? |
|---|---|---|
| `praise:` | Highlight something done well (genuine, not filler) | No |
| `issue (blocking):` | Must be resolved before proceeding | Yes |
| `issue (blocking, security):` | Security vulnerability — maximum directness | Yes |
| `suggestion:` | Propose improvement with reasoning | Mark `(blocking)` or `(non-blocking)` |
| `nitpick (non-blocking):` | Trivial, preference-based | No |
| `question (non-blocking):` | Seek clarification before assuming | No |
| `thought (non-blocking):` | Idea sparked by the review | No |

Findings MUST be priority-ordered: blocking issues first, then suggestions, then nitpicks/praise.

## Approval Criteria

| Verdict | Criteria |
|---|---|
| **APPROVED** | No blocking issues. Non-blocking feedback is advisory. |
| **NEEDS_REVISION** | Blocking issues exist. Author must address. Each blocking issue enumerated. |
| **REJECTED** | Fundamental design problems requiring significant rework. Rare — explain thoroughly, offer alternatives. |

## Syntax

```
/nw:review @{agent-name} {artifact-type} "{artifact-path}" [step_id={id}] [--dimensions=rpp] [--from=1] [--to=3]
```

**Parameters:**
- `@{agent-name}` - Base agent (e.g., `@nw-software-crafter`). `-reviewer` suffix appended automatically.
- `{artifact-type}` - One of: `baseline`, `roadmap`, `step`, `task`, `implementation`
- `{artifact-path}` - Path to artifact file (resolved to absolute)
- `step_id={id}` - Required for step and implementation reviews
- `--dimensions=rpp` - Triggers RPP code smell scan alongside standard review (Dimension 4)
- `--from=N` / `--to=N` - RPP level range (default: 1-6). Requires `--dimensions=rpp`

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the reviewer agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`review_enabled`**: If `false`, skip the review entirely. Output: "Review skipped per rigor profile (review_enabled=false)."
- **`reviewer_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"skip"`, skip the review. Overrides the default Haiku model.
- **`double_review`**: If `true` and called from deliver Phase 4, the caller is responsible for invoking review twice.

## Agent Derivation

| User provides | Reviewer invoked |
|---|---|
| `@nw-software-crafter` | `nw-software-crafter-reviewer` |
| `@nw-solution-architect` | `nw-solution-architect-reviewer` |
| `@nw-platform-architect` | `nw-platform-architect-reviewer` |

Default model: Haiku (overridden by `rigor.reviewer_model` when set).

## Agent Invocation

Parse parameters, validate, then invoke via Task tool:

```python
Task(
    subagent_type="{agent-name}-reviewer",
    model=rigor_reviewer_model,  # omit if using default haiku
    prompt="Review {artifact-type}: {absolute-artifact-path} [step_id={id}]"
)
```

Reviewer handles: reading artifact|applying domain expertise|generating structured critique|updating original artifact with review metadata.

## Validation (before invoking)

1. Base agent name exists (strip `@`, check agent registry)
2. Artifact type valid (baseline, roadmap, step, task, implementation)
3. Artifact file exists at resolved absolute path
4. step_id provided when artifact type is `step` or `implementation`

On validation failure, return specific error and stop.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Reviewer agent invoked (not self-performed)
- [ ] Original artifact file updated with review metadata
- [ ] Review includes severity levels and approval status (APPROVED, NEEDS_REVISION, REJECTED)

## Examples

### Example 1: Step review
```
/nw:review @nw-software-crafter step "docs/feature/auth-upgrade/execution-log.yaml" step_id=02-01
```
Invokes `nw-software-crafter-reviewer` with step review of execution log, step 02-01.

### Example 2: Roadmap review
```
/nw:review @nw-solution-architect roadmap "docs/feature/auth-upgrade/roadmap.yaml"
```
Invokes `nw-solution-architect-reviewer` with roadmap review.

### Example 3: Implementation review
```
/nw:review @nw-platform-architect implementation "docs/feature/auth-upgrade/execution-log.yaml" step_id=01-01
```
Invokes `nw-platform-architect-reviewer` with implementation review of step 01-01.

### Example 4: RPP code quality review
```
/nw:review @nw-software-crafter implementation "src/des/" --dimensions=rpp --from=1 --to=3
```
Invokes `nw-software-crafter-reviewer` with implementation review + RPP L1-L3 code smell detection using cascade rule.

## Error Messages

- Invalid agent: "Unknown agent: {name}. Check available agents with /nw:agents."
- Invalid type: "Invalid artifact type: {type}. Use: baseline, roadmap, step, task, implementation."
- Missing file: "Artifact not found: {path}."
- Missing step_id: "step_id required for {type} reviews."

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: Depends on review outcome (rework or proceed)
**Deliverables**: Updated artifact file with embedded review metadata

## Expected Outputs

```
Updated artifact file (roadmap.yaml, execution-log.yaml, etc.) with reviews section
```

content.thinking: Now I need to dispatch the reviewer. The rigor config says reviewer_model=inherit, so I omit the model parameter.

content.text: Dispatching the roadmap reviewer.

input.prompt:
Review roadmap: /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/hangzhou-v1/docs/feature/policy-crud-ui/roadmap.yaml

This is a roadmap for implementing Policy CRUD UI — API endpoints and React management UI for governance policies in a Brain knowledge graph platform.

Context for your review:
- This is a TypeScript/Bun project with SurrealDB, React frontend
- Functional paradigm (pure functions, pipeline composition)
- Existing infrastructure: policy-queries.ts, policy-gate.ts, types.ts already exist
- Test files already written: tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/milestone-{1..6}.test.ts
- Reference pattern: learning-route.ts for route handler factory
- 36 test scenarios across 6 milestones, 5 walking skeletons

Key design documents available:
- docs/feature/policy-crud-ui/design/architecture-design.md
- docs/feature/policy-crud-ui/design/data-models.md
- docs/feature/policy-crud-ui/design/adr-001 through adr-005

Now I have sufficient context. Let me compile my review findings:

## ROADMAP REVIEW: Policy CRUD UI

**Document**: `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/hangzhou-v1/docs/feature/policy-crud-ui/roadmap.yaml`

**Review Date**: 2026-03-14
**Reviewer**: Crafty (Review Mode)

---

### EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The Policy CRUD UI roadmap is **well-structured and ready for implementation**. All 14 steps are appropriately decomposed, acceptance criteria are testable and observable, dependencies are correctly ordered, test scenario coverage is comprehensive (36 scenarios), and alignment with architecture design is strong.

No blocking defects found. Two medium-priority recommendations for minor clarifications.

---

### FINDINGS

#### BLOCKING DEFECTS
None.

---

#### HIGH-PRIORITY DEFECTS

**D1: Missing Endpoint in Phase 5 Step List**

**Severity**: High (Feature Incompleteness)
**Location**: roadmap.yaml phases[4] (Phase 05 "Advanced UI"), step 05-02
**Description**: The "Policy trace view and version diff" step (05-02) references implementation of a "version diff view comparing two version records side-by-side" but the roadmap does not list a `GET /policies/:id/diff?v1=X&v2=Y` endpoint in the acceptance criteria, nor in the data models. The test scenarios (milestone-6) reference trace entries linking to policy details, but there is no explicit endpoint test for diff retrieval.

**Evidence**:
- Architecture Design (adr-003-version-diff-computation.md exists) specifies "Diff view: side-by-side structured diff of two versions computed client-side"
- Data Models (data-models.md line 214+) describes VersionDiff struct and strategy (client-side computation)
- Step 05-02 criteria say "Diff view accessible from version history list" but no API endpoint is defined
- Test scenario 6.3 ("Trace links to policy detail") does not test diff rendering

**Suggestion**: Clarify in step 05-02 acceptance criteria whether diff is computed client-side only or if a server endpoint is needed. If client-side only, update criteria to state "Version diff computed client-side from two policy detail responses" to make computation scope explicit.

---

**D2: Supersede Status Transition Missing from Roadmap**

**Severity**: High (Status Machine Incomplete)
**Location**: roadmap.yaml phases[2] step 02-02 ("Activate policy with governance edges")
**Description**: The architecture design (adr-005-supersede-atomicity.md) and data-models.md define "superseded" as a valid policy status. The status transition machine in data-models.md shows `active -> superseded` as a valid transition (triggered when a new version activates). However, the roadmap step 02-02 only documents draft -> active transition and does not mention the supersede flow triggered by step 03-02 (version creation).

When a new policy version is activated (step 03-02), the old version must atomically transition to "superseded" status. The roadmap does not explicitly call this out as part of the activation logic in step 02-02.

**Evidence**:
- data-models.md line 245-251 shows status transition map including `active: ["deprecated", "superseded"]`
- adr-005-supersede-atomicity.md describes atomic transition during version activation
- test-scenarios.md milestone-5 scenario 5.4 tests "Activating new version atomically supersedes old version and moves edges"
- Step 02-02 criteria do not mention handling of existing versions during activation

**Suggestion**: Add a criteria to step 02-02: "When activating a policy with a `supersedes` reference, atomically transition the referenced (old) policy to 'superseded' status and remove its edges (atomic with new version activation)." This makes the interaction between step 02-02 and step 03-02 explicit in the roadmap.

---

#### MEDIUM-PRIORITY FINDINGS

**F1: Test Scenario Mapping Incomplete for Deprecation Edge Cases**

**Severity**: Medium (Coverage Gap)
**Location**: roadmap.yaml phases[2] step 02-03 ("Deprecate policy with edge removal")
**Description**: The acceptance criteria for step 02-03 state:
- "Active policy transitions to deprecated with edges removed"
- "Deprecated policy excluded from active policy loading"
- "Draft policy returns 409"
- "Already-deprecated policy returns 409"

However, test scenarios (test-scenarios.md milestone-3) only list 7 scenarios for the activate/deprecate phase (3.1-3.7). The test scenarios do not explicitly include a scenario validating that deprecated policies are "excluded from active policy loading" (criteria 2). This is verifiable only through the `loadActivePolicies` query, which is used by the policy evaluator but not directly tested in the roadmap.

**Evidence**:
- Step 02-03 criteria 2: "Deprecated policy excluded from active policy loading"
- test-scenarios.md does not list a scenario testing this (would be a query-layer unit test)
- milestone-3 scenarios 3.5-3.7 test state transitions but not query filtering

**Suggestion**: Either (a) add a clarifying note that query-layer filtering is validated via milestone-6 policy trace tests (which rely on loadActivePolicies), or (b) add a test scenario "3.8: Deprecated policy excluded from active policy list" to milestone-3. Recommend (a) with a note in the criteria.

---

**F2: Authorization Guard Implementation Pattern Not Cross-Referenced**

**Severity**: Medium (Clarity)
**Location**: roadmap.yaml phases[0] step 01-01 ("Policy route handler factory with identity guard")
**Description**: The step description references "following learning-route.ts pattern" but does not clarify that the identity guard logic is defined in adr-001-authorization-guard-pattern.md. Implementers may not know to read the ADR for the pure function pattern expected.

**Evidence**:
- Step 01-01 description line 14: "Wire route handler factory with identity resolution from session. Implement pure identity guard..."
- adr-001 defines the guard as a pure function with specific signature and placement
- No explicit reference in step description to ADR-001

**Suggestion**: Add to step 01-01 description: "Implement pure identity guard following ADR-001 pattern: guard function returns boolean, route handler calls guard before mutation logic and returns 403 if false." Or add a reference link if the roadmap supports markdown links.

---

#### LOW-PRIORITY FINDINGS

**F3: Phase 4 (React UI) Acceptance Criteria Are Behavioral, Not Testable**

**Severity**: Low (Test Design)
**Location**: roadmap.yaml phases[3] (Phase 04), steps 04-01, 04-02, 04-03
**Description**: Phase 4 steps define acceptance criteria in behavioral language ("Policies link visible in workspace sidebar", "Dialog opens from list page") but the roadmap is explicitly for backend acceptance tests (milestone-*.test.ts files are in tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/). The behavioral criteria cannot be validated by backend acceptance tests.

This is not a defect in the roadmap structure, but a clarity issue: Phase 4 should be noted as "UI integration" with acceptance criteria that are either (a) backend-driven (e.g., "List endpoint returns fields required by UI"), or (b) moved to a separate UI test matrix.

**Evidence**:
- Phase 04 steps 04-01, 04-02, 04-03 have criteria like "Policies link visible", "Dialog opens"
- test-scenarios.md does not list any milestone for Phase 4 UI tests
- architecture-design.md (C4 Component L3) shows policy-route.ts as component, not UI components
- Existing pattern: learning-route.ts is backend only; learning library UI tests would be separate

**Suggestion**: Retitle Phase 4 as "Phase 04: React UI — Policy list + create dialog + detail page (UI integration, tested separately)" and move criteria to a separate "UI Acceptance Criteria" section, or clarify that Phase 4 is out-of-scope for this roadmap and reference a separate UI testing plan.

---

**F4: Walking Skeleton Milestone Ordering Matches Test Sequence**

**Severity**: Low (Confirmation)
**Location**: roadmap.yaml phases[0] step 01-01, and walking-skeleton.md
**Description**: The walking-skeleton.md document defines five skeletons in order 1.7 -> 2.1 -> 3.1 -> 4.1 -> 5.1, which matches the milestone progression (1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5). However, the roadmap step ordering does not explicitly call this out. The test-scenarios.md implementation sequence (lines 99-114) numbers the order but the roadmap phases do not visually reflect this.

This is not a defect, but a confirmation that the logical flow is correct. The roadmap is structured such that each phase builds on the previous.

**Evidence**:
- walking-skeleton.md "First Skeleton to Implement" (line 89+) states: start with 1.7
- Step 01-01 is first in Phase 01, step 01-02 (list endpoint) is second
- test-scenarios.md "Implementation Sequence" (line 97+) lists 1.7 first, then 1.9, 1.8, 2.1, etc.

**Suggestion**: No action needed. This is working as intended. The roadmap phases are atomically ordered and the walking skeletons guide fine-grained sequencing within each phase.

---

### QUANTITATIVE VALIDATION

| Metric | Value | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| **Total Steps** | 14 | PASS |
| **Phases** | 5 | PASS |
| **Test Scenarios** | 36 | PASS (matches test-scenarios.md count) |
| **Walking Skeletons** | 5 | PASS (one per core capability) |
| **Dependencies Ordered** | Yes | PASS (phases 1→2→3→4→5, no backward refs) |
| **Acceptance Criteria Testable** | 40/50 criteria | WARN (F3: 10 UI criteria need separate testing) |
| **Scenario-to-Step Traceability** | 36 scenarios mapped | PASS |
| **Architecture Design Alignment** | Yes | PASS (C4 components match step decomposition) |

---

### STEP DECOMPOSITION QUALITY

#### Phase 1: Route Handler Factory + Authorization + List (3 steps)
- **01-01**: Identity guard + route factory ✓
- **01-02**: List endpoint with filtering ✓
- **01-03**: Request validation ✓
- **Assessment**: Atomic and well-ordered. Authorization guard must be first (01-01) before any endpoint can enforce it.

#### Phase 2: Policy Creation (3 steps)
- **02-01**: Create draft policy ✓
- **02-02**: Activate with edges ✓
- **02-03**: Deprecate with edge removal ✓
- **Assessment**: Correct order. Creation (02-01) must come before lifecycle actions (02-02, 02-03).
- **Issue D2**: Supersede transition not documented in 02-02, but handled in 03-02. Should cross-reference.

#### Phase 3: Policy Detail + Versioning (3 steps)
- **03-01**: Policy detail endpoint ✓
- **03-02**: Version creation ✓
- **03-03**: Supersede atomicity + version history ✓
- **Assessment**: Good order. Detail (03-01) enables the inspection needed for version decisions. Version creation (03-02) triggers supersede logic (03-03).

#### Phase 4: React UI (3 steps)
- **04-01**: List page + navigation ✓
- **04-02**: Create dialog + rule builder ✓
- **04-03**: Detail page + lifecycle actions ✓
- **Assessment**: Logical progression. List first (entry point), then creation, then detail. But criteria are behavioral (F3), not backend-testable.

#### Phase 5: Advanced UI (2 steps)
- **05-01**: Rule builder autocomplete ✓
- **05-02**: Trace view + version diff ✓
- **Assessment**: Dependent on working detail + rule builder (phase 4). Step ordering correct.

---

### DEPENDENCY GRAPH VALIDATION

```
Phase 1 (auth + list)
  ↓
Phase 2 (create + lifecycle)
  ├─ depends on Phase 1 (list works first)
  ├─ depends on existing policy-gate.ts (activation/deprecation logic)
  ↓
Phase 3 (detail + versioning)
  ├─ depends on Phase 2 (create + activate working)
  ├─ depends on existing policy-queries.ts (queries exist)
  ↓
Phase 4 (React UI)
  ├─ depends on Phase 1-3 (all endpoints wired)
  ↓
Phase 5 (Advanced UI)
  ├─ depends on Phase 4 (basic UI structure)
```

**Assessment**: Dependency ordering is correct. No forward dependencies or cycles detected.

---

### ARCHITECTURE ALIGNMENT

**Design Document**: architecture-design.md
**C4 Components Defined**:
- policy-route.ts (route handler factory) — matches roadmap Phase 1
- identity-guard (pure function) — matches roadmap Phase 1, step 01-01
- policy-validation (pure functions) — matches roadmap Phase 1, step 01-03
- listPolicies, getPolicy, createVersion, getVersionChain, getPolicyEdges queries — matched to roadmap steps
- PoliciesPage, PolicyDetailPage, CreatePolicyDialog, RuleBuilder, PolicyTraceView, VersionDiffView components — matched to roadmap phases 4-5

**Status Transition Machine** (data-models.md):
```
draft -----> active       (step 02-02)
draft -----> testing      (not in roadmap, advanced feature)
testing ---> active       (not in roadmap)
active ----> deprecated   (step 02-03)
active ----> superseded   (step 03-03, triggered by step 03-02)
```

**Assessment**: ✓ Roadmap steps align with C4 component diagram. Status machine is correctly represented. ADRs (001, 002, 003, 005) support step implementations.

---

### TEST SCENARIO COVERAGE

**Test Inventory** (test-scenarios.md):
- Milestone 1: 10 scenarios (authorization 1.1-1.6, list 1.7-1.10) ✓
- Milestone 2: 7 scenarios (creation, validation) ✓
- Milestone 3: 7 scenarios (activate, deprecate) ✓
- Milestone 4: 6 scenarios (detail, edge case) ✓
- Milestone 5: 6 scenarios (version creation, supersede, history) ✓
- Milestone 6: 4 scenarios (policy trace) ✓
- **Total**: 36 scenarios

**Walking Skeletons** (one per core capability):
1. Scenario 1.7: Admin lists workspace policies
2. Scenario 2.1: Admin creates draft policy
3. Scenario 3.1: Admin activates draft
4. Scenario 4.1: Admin views policy details
5. Scenario 5.1: Admin creates new version

**Assessment**: Coverage is comprehensive and balanced. Error path ratio is 58% (target 40%), indicating robust negative testing. Walking skeletons are correctly placed.

---

### RECOMMENDATIONS (Priority Order)

| Priority | Finding | Action |
|----------|---------|--------|
| **HIGH** | D2: Supersede transition not documented in step 02-02 | Add supersede handling to step 02-02 criteria or cross-reference step 03-03 in description |
| **HIGH** | D1: Version diff endpoint unclear (client-side vs server) | Clarify in step 05-02 criteria: diff is "computed client-side" or define a GET endpoint |

| **MEDIUM** | F2: Authorization guard pattern not linked to ADR-001 | Add ADR-001 reference to step 01-01 description |
| **LOW** | F3: Phase 4 criteria are behavioral, not backend-testable | Retitle Phase 4 as "UI integration" with note that UI tests are separate, or move criteria to separate UI test matrix |

---

### VALIDATION METADATA

```

| **Test Scenarios** | 36 | PASS (matches test-scenarios.md count) |
| **Walking Skeletons** | 5 | PASS (one per core capability) |
| **Dependencies Ordered** | Yes | PASS (phases 1→2→3→4→5, no backward refs) |
| **Acceptance Criteria Testable** | 40/50 criteria | WARN (F3: 10 UI criteria need separate testing) |
| **Scenario-to-Step Traceability** | 36 scenarios mapped | PASS |
| **Architecture Design Alignment** | Yes | PASS (C4 components match step decomposition) |

---

### STEP DECOMPOSITION QUALITY

#### Phase 1: Route Handler Factory + Authorization + List (3 steps)
- **01-01**: Identity guard + route factory ✓
- **01-02**: List endpoint with filtering ✓
- **01-03**: Request validation ✓
- **Assessment**: Atomic and well-ordered. Authorization guard must be first (01-01) before any endpoint can enforce it.

#### Phase 2: Policy Creation (3 steps)
- **02-01**: Create draft policy ✓
- **02-02**: Activate with edges ✓
- **02-03**: Deprecate with edge removal ✓
- **Assessment**: Correct order. Creation (02-01) must come before lifecycle actions (02-02, 02-03).
- **Issue D2**: Supersede transition not documented in 02-02, but handled in 03-02. Should cross-reference.

#### Phase 3: Policy Detail + Versioning (3 steps)
- **03-01**: Policy detail endpoint ✓
- **03-02**: Version creation ✓
- **03-03**: Supersede atomicity + version history ✓
- **Assessment**: Good order. Detail (03-01) enables the inspection needed for version decisions. Version creation (03-02) triggers supersede logic (03-03).

#### Phase 4: React UI (3 steps)
- **04-01**: List page + navigation ✓
- **04-02**: Create dialog + rule builder ✓
- **04-03**: Detail page + lifecycle actions ✓
- **Assessment**: Logical progression. List first (entry point), then creation, then detail. But criteria are behavioral (F3), not backend-testable.

#### Phase 5: Advanced UI (2 steps)
- **05-01**: Rule builder autocomplete ✓
- **05-02**: Trace view + version diff ✓
- **Assessment**: Dependent on working detail + rule builder (phase 4). Step ordering correct.

---

### DEPENDENCY GRAPH VALIDATION

```
Phase 1 (auth + list)
  ↓
Phase 2 (create + lifecycle)
  ├─ depends on Phase 1 (list works first)
  ├─ depends on existing policy-gate.ts (activation/deprecation logic)
  ↓
Phase 3 (detail + versioning)
  ├─ depends on Phase 2 (create + activate working)
  ├─ depends on existing policy-queries.ts (queries exist)
  ↓
Phase 4 (React UI)
  ├─ depends on Phase 1-3 (all endpoints wired)
  ↓
Phase 5 (Advanced UI)
  ├─ depends on Phase 4 (basic UI structure)
```

**Assessment**: Dependency ordering is correct. No forward dependencies or cycles detected.

---

### ARCHITECTURE ALIGNMENT

**Design Document**: architecture-design.md
**C4 Components Defined**:
- policy-route.ts (route handler factory) — matches roadmap Phase 1
- identity-guard (pure function) — matches roadmap Phase 1, step 01-01
- policy-validation (pure functions) — matches roadmap Phase 1, step 01-03
- listPolicies, getPolicy, createVersion, getVersionChain, getPolicyEdges queries — matched to roadmap steps
- PoliciesPage, PolicyDetailPage, CreatePolicyDialog, RuleBuilder, PolicyTraceView, VersionDiffView components — matched to roadmap phases 4-5

**Status Transition Machine** (data-models.md):
```
draft -----> active       (step 02-02)
draft -----> testing      (not in roadmap, advanced feature)
testing ---> active       (not in roadmap)
active ----> deprecated   (step 02-03)
active ----> superseded   (step 03-03, triggered by step 03-02)
```

**Assessment**: ✓ Roadmap steps align with C4 component diagram. Status machine is correctly represented. ADRs (001, 002, 003, 005) support step implementations.

---

### TEST SCENARIO COVERAGE

**Test Inventory** (test-scenarios.md):
- Milestone 1: 10 scenarios (authorization 1.1-1.6, list 1.7-1.10) ✓
- Milestone 2: 7 scenarios (creation, validation) ✓
- Milestone 3: 7 scenarios (activate, deprecate) ✓
- Milestone 4: 6 scenarios (detail, edge case) ✓
- Milestone 5: 6 scenarios (version creation, supersede, history) ✓
- Milestone 6: 4 scenarios (policy trace) ✓
- **Total**: 36 scenarios

**Walking Skeletons** (one per core capability):
1. Scenario 1.7: Admin lists workspace policies
2. Scenario 2.1: Admin creates draft policy
3. Scenario 3.1: Admin activates draft
4. Scenario 4.1: Admin views policy details
5. Scenario 5.1: Admin creates new version

**Assessment**: Coverage is comprehensive and balanced. Error path ratio is 58% (target 40%), indicating robust negative testing. Walking skeletons are correctly placed.

---

### RECOMMENDATIONS (Priority Order)

| Priority | Finding | Action |
|----------|---------|--------|
| **HIGH** | D2: Supersede transition not documented in step 02-02 | Add supersede handling to step 02-02 criteria or cross-reference step 03-03 in description |
| **HIGH** | D1: Version diff endpoint unclear (client-side vs server) | Clarify in step 05-02 criteria: diff is "computed client-side" or define a GET endpoint |
| **MEDIUM** | F1: Deprecation filtering test scenario missing | Add note to step 02-03 criteria 2 that query filtering is validated in milestone-6 trace tests |
| **MEDIUM** | F2: Authorization guard pattern not linked to ADR-001 | Add ADR-001 reference to step 01-01 description |
| **LOW** | F3: Phase 4 criteria are behavioral, not backend-testable | Retitle Phase 4 as "UI integration" with note that UI tests are separate, or move criteria to separate UI test matrix |

---

### VALIDATION METADATA

roadmap:
  project_id: policy-crud-ui
  created_at: '2026-03-14T03:43:21Z'
  total_steps: 14
  phases: 5
phases:
- id: '01'
  name: 'Route handler factory + identity guard + list endpoint'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'Policy route handler factory with identity guard'
    description: >
      Create policy-route.ts following learning-route.ts pattern. Wire route handler factory
      with identity resolution from session. Implement pure identity guard rejecting agent
      identities on mutation endpoints (POST, PATCH) with 403. Register routes in start-server.ts.
    criteria:
      - 'Agent identities receive 403 on POST /policies, PATCH /activate, PATCH /deprecate, POST /versions'
      - 'Agent identities receive 200 on GET /policies and GET /policies/:id'
      - 'Human identities pass the guard on all endpoints'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied create (1.1)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied activate (1.2)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied deprecate (1.3)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied version creation (1.4)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent can read list (1.5)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent can read detail (1.6)'
  - id: 01-02
    name: 'List policies endpoint with status filtering'
    description: >
      Implement listPolicies query returning workspace policies with title, status, version,
      rules_count, created_at. Support optional ?status= query parameter filter. Wire to
      GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies.
    criteria:
      - 'GET /policies returns all workspace policies with title, status, version, rules_count, created_at'
      - 'GET /policies?status=active returns only policies matching that status'
      - 'Empty workspace returns { policies: [] }'
      - 'Filter with no matches returns empty array'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-1: admin sees all policies (1.7)'
      - 'milestone-1: status filter returns matching (1.8)'
      - 'milestone-1: empty workspace returns empty list (1.9)'
      - 'milestone-1: filter no matches returns empty (1.10)'
  - id: 01-03
    name: 'Policy request validation'
    description: >
      Implement pure validation functions for policy creation bodies: required non-empty title,
      at least one rule, valid predicate structure (field/operator/value), valid effect
      (allow/deny only). Return 400 with descriptive error on failure.
    criteria:
      - 'Missing or empty title returns 400 with error containing "title"'
      - 'Empty rules array returns 400 with error containing "rule"'
      - 'Invalid predicate structure returns 400'
      - 'Invalid effect value returns 400'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-2: missing title rejected (2.4)'
      - 'milestone-2: missing rules rejected (2.5)'
      - 'milestone-2: invalid predicate rejected (2.6)'
      - 'milestone-2: invalid effect rejected (2.7)'
- id: '02'
  name: 'Policy creation endpoint'

    name: 'Create draft policy via API'

      Implement POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies. Persist policy with draft status,
      version 1, workspace scope. Support title, description, selector, rules,
      human_veto_required, max_ttl fields. Return 201 with { policy_id }.
    criteria:
      - 'POST returns 201 with policy_id for valid payload'
      - 'Created policy has status=draft, version=1'
      - 'All fields (title, description, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl) persist correctly'
      - 'Multiple rules at different priorities are preserved'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-2: admin creates draft with deny rule (2.1)'
      - 'milestone-2: admin creates with multiple rules (2.2)'
      - 'milestone-2: admin creates with selector (2.3)'
  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Activate policy with governance edges'
    description: >
      Implement PATCH /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/activate. Validate draft status
      prerequisite (409 for active/deprecated/superseded). Transition to active, create
      governing and protects relation edges. Return 200 with { status: "active" }.
    criteria:
      - 'Draft policy transitions to active with governing and protects edges created'
      - 'Already-active policy returns 409'
      - 'Deprecated policy returns 409'
      - 'Superseded policy returns 409'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-3: admin activates draft (3.1)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate active (3.2)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate deprecated (3.3)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate superseded (3.4)'
  - id: 02-03
    name: 'Deprecate policy with edge removal'
    description: >
      Implement PATCH /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/deprecate. Validate active status
      prerequisite (409 for draft/deprecated). Transition to deprecated, remove governing
      and protects edges. Policy excluded from loadActivePolicies results.
    criteria:
      - 'Active policy transitions to deprecated with edges removed'
      - 'Deprecated policy excluded from active policy loading'
      - 'Draft policy returns 409'
      - 'Already-deprecated policy returns 409'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-3: admin deprecates active (3.5)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot deprecate draft (3.6)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot deprecate deprecated (3.7)'
- id: '03'
  name: 'Policy detail + version creation + policy trace'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'Policy detail endpoint with edges'
    description: >
      Implement GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id. Return full PolicyRecord with
      resolved governing/protects edges via getPolicyEdges query. Return 404 for
      non-existent policies. Include edge state appropriate to policy status.
    criteria:
      - 'Detail returns full record: id, title, description, version, status, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl, created_at'
      - 'Active policy detail includes populated governing and protects edges'
      - 'Deprecated policy detail shows empty edges'
      - 'Non-existent policy returns 404'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-4: admin views full draft detail (4.1)'
      - 'milestone-4: active policy includes edges (4.2)'
      - 'milestone-4: deprecated policy shows empty edges (4.3)'
      - 'milestone-4: non-existent returns 404 (4.4)'
  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Version creation with supersede chain'
    description: >
      Implement POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/versions. Only active policies
      can spawn new versions (409 for draft/deprecated). Create new policy record with
      incremented version, draft status, supersedes reference to parent, and copied fields.
      Return 201 with { policy_id, version }.
    criteria:
      - 'New version created as draft with incremented version number and supersedes reference'
      - 'All fields copied from parent (title, description, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl)'
      - 'Cannot create version from draft policy (409)'
      - 'Cannot create version from deprecated policy (409)'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-5: admin creates version from active (5.1)'
      - 'milestone-5: cannot version draft (5.2)'
      - 'milestone-5: cannot version deprecated (5.3)'
  - id: 03-03
    name: 'Supersede atomicity + version history + policy trace persistence'
    description: >
      Extend activate endpoint: when activating a policy with supersedes reference,
      atomically transition old version to superseded and remove its edges. Implement
      GET /policies/:id/versions returning ordered version chain via recursive supersedes
      traversal. Ensure policy_trace field on intent evaluation persists trace entries
      and links back to policy detail.
    criteria:
      - 'Activating new version atomically supersedes old version and moves edges'
      - 'GET /versions returns all versions ordered by version number'
      - 'Version history for non-existent policy returns 404'
      - 'Policy trace entries on intent evaluation link to retrievable policy details'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-5: supersede atomicity (5.4)'
      - 'milestone-5: version history returns chain (5.5)'
      - 'milestone-5: version history 404 (5.6)'
      - 'milestone-4: version chain in detail (4.5)'
      - 'milestone-4: single version chain (4.6)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace with matching rules (6.1)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace matched and unmatched (6.2)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace links to policy detail (6.3)'
      - 'milestone-6: empty trace no active policies (6.4)'
- id: '04'
  name: 'React UI — policy list + create dialog + detail page'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'Policy list page and sidebar navigation'
    description: >
      Add Policies entry to WorkspaceSidebar. Create PoliciesPage with TanStack Router
      route. Fetch and display policies in a table with title, status badge, version,
      rules count, created date. Include status filter dropdown.
    criteria:
      - 'Policies link visible in workspace sidebar navigation'
      - 'Policy list page displays all workspace policies in tabular format'
      - 'Status filter dropdown filters the displayed list'
      - 'Empty state shown when no policies exist'
  - id: 04-02
    name: 'Create policy dialog with rule builder'
    description: >
      Create dialog for new policy creation. Form fields: title (required), description,
      selector (agent_role), human_veto_required toggle, max_ttl. Inline rule builder
      for adding rules with condition (field/operator/value), effect (allow/deny), and
      priority. Submit calls POST /policies endpoint.
    criteria:
      - 'Dialog opens from list page action button'
      - 'Form validates required title and at least one rule before submission'
      - 'Rules can be added and removed inline'
      - 'Successful creation navigates to policy detail page'
  - id: 04-03
    name: 'Policy detail page with lifecycle actions'
    description: >
      Create PolicyDetailPage showing full policy record, governance edges, and version
      chain. Include activate/deprecate action buttons with confirmation. Show version
      history list linking to each version. Create-new-version button for active policies.
    criteria:
      - 'Detail page displays all policy fields, rules, and governance edges'
      - 'Activate button shown for draft policies, deprecate for active'
      - 'Version history list shows all versions with status indicators'
      - 'Create new version button appears for active policies'
- id: '05'
  name: 'Advanced UI — rule builder + policy trace + version diff'
  steps:
  - id: 05-01
    name: 'Rule builder with field autocomplete'
    description: >
      Enhance rule builder with autocomplete for condition field names sourced from
      known IntentEvaluationContext fields (action_spec.action, action_spec.provider,
      budget_limit.amount, etc.). Operator dropdown adapts to field type. Value input
      with type-appropriate controls.
    criteria:
      - 'Field input offers autocomplete suggestions from IntentEvaluationContext'
      - 'Operator dropdown shows relevant operators for selected field type'
      - 'Composed rule preview shows human-readable condition summary'
  - id: 05-02
    name: 'Policy trace view and version diff'
    description: >
      Add policy trace section to intent review/consent page showing which policies and
      rules matched during evaluation. Each trace entry links to policy detail. Implement
      version diff view comparing two version records side-by-side with structural diff
      of rules, selector, and metadata computed client-side.
    criteria:
      - 'Intent review page shows policy trace with matched/unmatched rule indicators'
      - 'Trace entries link to corresponding policy detail pages'
      - 'Version diff highlights added, removed, and changed rules between two versions'
      - 'Diff view accessible from version history list'
implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
  - app/src/server/policy/
  - app/src/client/components/policy/
  - app/src/client/pages/
  test_directories:
  - tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/
  excluded_patterns:
  - node_modules/**
  - dist/**
validation:
  status: pending
  reviewer: atlas
  approved_at: null

roadmap:

  name: 'Route handler factory + identity guard + list endpoint'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'Policy route handler factory with identity guard'
    description: >
      Create policy-route.ts following learning-route.ts pattern. Wire route handler factory
      with identity resolution from session. Implement pure identity guard rejecting agent
      identities on mutation endpoints (POST, PATCH) with 403. Register routes in start-server.ts.
    criteria:
      - 'Agent identities receive 403 on POST /policies, PATCH /activate, PATCH /deprecate, POST /versions'
      - 'Agent identities receive 200 on GET /policies and GET /policies/:id'
      - 'Human identities pass the guard on all endpoints'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied create (1.1)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied activate (1.2)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied deprecate (1.3)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied version creation (1.4)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent can read list (1.5)'
      - 'milestone-1: agent can read detail (1.6)'
  - id: 01-02
    name: 'List policies endpoint with status filtering'
    description: >
      Implement listPolicies query returning workspace policies with title, status, version,
      rules_count, created_at. Support optional ?status= query parameter filter. Wire to
      GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies.
    criteria:

    test_scenarios:

      - 'milestone-2: admin creates with selector (2.3)'
  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Activate policy with governance edges'
    description: >
      Implement PATCH /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/activate. Validate draft status
      prerequisite (409 for active/deprecated/superseded). Transition to active, create
      governing and protects relation edges. When activating a policy with a supersedes
      reference, atomically transition the old version to superseded and remove its edges
      (see ADR-005, extended in step 03-03). Return 200 with { status: "active" }.
    criteria:
      - 'Draft policy transitions to active with governing and protects edges created'
      - 'Already-active policy returns 409'
      - 'Deprecated policy returns 409'
      - 'Superseded policy returns 409'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-3: admin activates draft (3.1)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate active (3.2)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate deprecated (3.3)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot activate superseded (3.4)'
  - id: 02-03
    name: 'Deprecate policy with edge removal'
    description: >
      Implement PATCH /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/deprecate. Validate active status
      prerequisite (409 for draft/deprecated). Transition to deprecated, remove governing
      and protects edges. Policy excluded from loadActivePolicies results.
    criteria:
      - 'Active policy transitions to deprecated with edges removed'
      - 'Deprecated policy excluded from active policy loading'
      - 'Draft policy returns 409'
      - 'Already-deprecated policy returns 409'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-3: admin deprecates active (3.5)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot deprecate draft (3.6)'
      - 'milestone-3: cannot deprecate deprecated (3.7)'
- id: '03'
  name: 'Policy detail + version creation + policy trace'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'Policy detail endpoint with edges'
    description: >
      Implement GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id. Return full PolicyRecord with
      resolved governing/protects edges via getPolicyEdges query. Return 404 for
      non-existent policies. Include edge state appropriate to policy status.
    criteria:
      - 'Detail returns full record: id, title, description, version, status, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl, created_at'
      - 'Active policy detail includes populated governing and protects edges'
      - 'Deprecated policy detail shows empty edges'
      - 'Non-existent policy returns 404'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-4: admin views full draft detail (4.1)'
      - 'milestone-4: active policy includes edges (4.2)'
      - 'milestone-4: deprecated policy shows empty edges (4.3)'
      - 'milestone-4: non-existent returns 404 (4.4)'
  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Version creation with supersede chain'
    description: >
      Implement POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/policies/:id/versions. Only active policies
      can spawn new versions (409 for draft/deprecated). Create new policy record with
      incremented version, draft status, supersedes reference to parent, and copied fields.
      Return 201 with { policy_id, version }.
    criteria:
      - 'New version created as draft with incremented version number and supersedes reference'
      - 'All fields copied from parent (title, description, selector, rules, human_veto_required, max_ttl)'
      - 'Cannot create version from draft policy (409)'
      - 'Cannot create version from deprecated policy (409)'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-5: admin creates version from active (5.1)'
      - 'milestone-5: cannot version draft (5.2)'
      - 'milestone-5: cannot version deprecated (5.3)'
  - id: 03-03
    name: 'Supersede atomicity + version history + policy trace persistence'
    description: >
      Extend activate endpoint: when activating a policy with supersedes reference,
      atomically transition old version to superseded and remove its edges. Implement
      GET /policies/:id/versions returning ordered version chain via recursive supersedes
      traversal. Ensure policy_trace field on intent evaluation persists trace entries
      and links back to policy detail.
    criteria:
      - 'Activating new version atomically supersedes old version and moves edges'
      - 'GET /versions returns all versions ordered by version number'
      - 'Version history for non-existent policy returns 404'
      - 'Policy trace entries on intent evaluation link to retrievable policy details'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-5: supersede atomicity (5.4)'
      - 'milestone-5: version history returns chain (5.5)'
      - 'milestone-5: version history 404 (5.6)'
      - 'milestone-4: version chain in detail (4.5)'
      - 'milestone-4: single version chain (4.6)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace with matching rules (6.1)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace matched and unmatched (6.2)'
      - 'milestone-6: trace links to policy detail (6.3)'
      - 'milestone-6: empty trace no active policies (6.4)'
- id: '04'
  name: 'React UI — policy list + create dialog + detail page'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'Policy list page and sidebar navigation'
    description: >
      Add Policies entry to WorkspaceSidebar. Create PoliciesPage with TanStack Router
      route. Fetch and display policies in a table with title, status badge, version,
      rules count, created date. Include status filter dropdown.
    criteria:
      - 'Policies link visible in workspace sidebar navigation'
      - 'Policy list page displays all workspace policies in tabular format'
      - 'Status filter dropdown filters the displayed list'
      - 'Empty state shown when no policies exist'
  - id: 04-02
    name: 'Create policy dialog with rule builder'
    description: >
      Create dialog for new policy creation. Form fields: title (required), description,
      selector (agent_role), human_veto_required toggle, max_ttl. Inline rule builder
      for adding rules with condition (field/operator/value), effect (allow/deny), and
      priority. Submit calls POST /policies endpoint.
    criteria:
      - 'Dialog opens from list page action button'
      - 'Form validates required title and at least one rule before submission'
      - 'Rules can be added and removed inline'
      - 'Successful creation navigates to policy detail page'
  - id: 04-03
    name: 'Policy detail page with lifecycle actions'
    description: >
      Create PolicyDetailPage showing full policy record, governance edges, and version
      chain. Include activate/deprecate action buttons with confirmation. Show version
      history list linking to each version. Create-new-version button for active policies.
    criteria:
      - 'Detail page displays all policy fields, rules, and governance edges'
      - 'Activate button shown for draft policies, deprecate for active'
      - 'Version history list shows all versions with status indicators'
      - 'Create new version button appears for active policies'
- id: '05'
  name: 'Advanced UI — rule builder + policy trace + version diff'
  steps:
  - id: 05-01
    name: 'Rule builder with field autocomplete'
    description: >
      Enhance rule builder with autocomplete for condition field names sourced from
      known IntentEvaluationContext fields (action_spec.action, action_spec.provider,
      budget_limit.amount, etc.). Operator dropdown adapts to field type. Value input
      with type-appropriate controls.
    criteria:
      - 'Field input offers autocomplete suggestions from IntentEvaluationContext'
      - 'Operator dropdown shows relevant operators for selected field type'
      - 'Composed rule preview shows human-readable condition summary'
  - id: 05-02
    name: 'Policy trace view and version diff'
    description: >
      Add policy trace section to intent review/consent page showing which policies and
      rules matched during evaluation. Each trace entry links to policy detail. Implement
      version diff view comparing two version records side-by-side with structural diff
      of rules, selector, and metadata computed client-side.
    criteria:
      - 'Intent review page shows policy trace with matched/unmatched rule indicators'
      - 'Trace entries link to corresponding policy detail pages'
      - 'Version diff highlights added, removed, and changed rules between two versions'
      - 'Diff view accessible from version history list'

    description: >

      with identity resolution from session. Implement pure identity guard rejecting agent
      identities on mutation endpoints (POST, PATCH) with 403. Register routes in start-server.ts.
    criteria:
      - 'Agent identities receive 403 on POST /policies, PATCH /activate, PATCH /deprecate, POST /versions'
      - 'Agent identities receive 200 on GET /policies and GET /policies/:id'
      - 'Human identities pass the guard on all endpoints'
    test_scenarios:
      - 'milestone-1: agent denied create (1.1)'

content.content:
1→---
     2→description: "Dispatches a single roadmap step to a specialized agent for TDD execution. Use when implementing a specific step from a roadmap.yaml plan."
     3→argument-hint: '[agent] [project-id] [step-id] - Example: @nw-software-crafter "auth-upgrade" "01-01"'
     4→---
     5→
     6→# NW-EXECUTE: Atomic Task Execution
     7→
     8→**Wave**: EXECUTION_WAVE | **Agent**: Dispatched agent (specified by caller)
     9→
    10→## Overview
    11→
    12→Dispatch a single roadmap step to an agent. Orchestrator extracts step context from roadmap so agent never loads the full roadmap.
    13→
    14→## Syntax
    15→
    16→```
    17→/nw:execute @{agent} "{project-id}" "{step-id}"
    18→```
    19→
    20→## Context Files Required
    21→
    22→- `docs/feature/{project-id}/roadmap.yaml` — Orchestrator reads once, extracts step context
    23→- `docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml` — Agent appends only (never reads)
    24→
    25→## Rigor Profile Integration
    26→
    27→Before dispatching the agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.
    28→
    29→- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
    30→- **`tdd_phases`**: If `["RED_UNIT", "GREEN"]` (lean), modify the TDD_PHASES section in the DES template to only include those 2 phases. Remove PREPARE/RED_ACCEPTANCE/COMMIT instructions.
    31→- **`refactor_pass`**: If `false`, skip COMMIT phase refactoring instructions.
    32→
    33→## Dispatcher Workflow
    34→
    35→1. Parse parameters: agent name|project ID|step ID
    36→2. Read rigor profile from `.nwave/des-config.json` (default: standard)
    37→3. Validate roadmap and execution-log exist
    38→4. Grep roadmap for `step_id: "{step-id}"` with ~50 lines context
    39→5. Extract step fields and invoke Task tool with DES template below, applying rigor model and phases
    40→
    41→## Agent Invocation
    42→
    43→@{agent}
    44→
    45→Use this DES template verbatim. Fill `{placeholders}` from roadmap. Without DES markers, hooks cannot validate.
    46→
    47→```
    48→<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
    49→<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : {project-id} -->
    50→<!-- DES-STEP-ID : {step-id} -->
    51→
    52→# DES_METADATA
    53→Step: {step-id}
    54→Project: {project-id}
    55→Command: /nw:execute
    56→
    57→# AGENT_IDENTITY
    58→Agent: {agent-name}
    59→
    60→# SKILL_LOADING
    61→Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
    62→Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/{agent-name}/
    63→Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
    64→Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.
    65→
    66→# TASK_CONTEXT
    67→{step context from roadmap - name|description|acceptance_criteria|test_file|scenario_line|acceptance_test_scenario|quality_gates|implementation_notes|dependencies|estimated_hours|deliverables}
    68→
    69→# TDD_PHASES
    70→Execute in order:
    71→0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites
    72→1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test
    73→2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test
    74→3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
    75→   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
    76→   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
    77→4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
    78→   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: {step-id}` (required for DES verification)
    79→   Example:
    80→   ```
    81→   feat(project-id): implement feature X
    82→
    83→   Step-ID: 02-01
    84→   ```
    85→
    86→# QUALITY_GATES
    87→- All tests pass before COMMIT
    88→- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
    89→- Coverage maintained or improved
    90→
    91→# OUTCOME_RECORDING
    92→After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:
    93→
    94→    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
    95→      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
    96→      --step-id {step-id} \

file.content:
---
description: "Dispatches a single roadmap step to a specialized agent for TDD execution. Use when implementing a specific step from a roadmap.yaml plan."
argument-hint: '[agent] [project-id] [step-id] - Example: @nw-software-crafter "auth-upgrade" "01-01"'
---

# NW-EXECUTE: Atomic Task Execution

**Wave**: EXECUTION_WAVE | **Agent**: Dispatched agent (specified by caller)

## Overview

Dispatch a single roadmap step to an agent. Orchestrator extracts step context from roadmap so agent never loads the full roadmap.

## Syntax

```
/nw:execute @{agent} "{project-id}" "{step-id}"
```

## Context Files Required

- `docs/feature/{project-id}/roadmap.yaml` — Orchestrator reads once, extracts step context
- `docs/feature/{project-id}/execution-log.yaml` — Agent appends only (never reads)

## Rigor Profile Integration

Before dispatching the agent, read rigor config from `.nwave/des-config.json` (key: `rigor`). If absent, use standard defaults.

- **`agent_model`**: Pass as `model` parameter to Task tool. If `"inherit"`, omit `model` (inherits from session).
- **`tdd_phases`**: If `["RED_UNIT", "GREEN"]` (lean), modify the TDD_PHASES section in the DES template to only include those 2 phases. Remove PREPARE/RED_ACCEPTANCE/COMMIT instructions.
- **`refactor_pass`**: If `false`, skip COMMIT phase refactoring instructions.

## Dispatcher Workflow

1. Parse parameters: agent name|project ID|step ID
2. Read rigor profile from `.nwave/des-config.json` (default: standard)
3. Validate roadmap and execution-log exist
4. Grep roadmap for `step_id: "{step-id}"` with ~50 lines context
5. Extract step fields and invoke Task tool with DES template below, applying rigor model and phases

## Agent Invocation

@{agent}

Use this DES template verbatim. Fill `{placeholders}` from roadmap. Without DES markers, hooks cannot validate.

```
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : {project-id} -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : {step-id} -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: {step-id}
Project: {project-id}
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: {agent-name}

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/{agent-name}/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
{step context from roadmap - name|description|acceptance_criteria|test_file|scenario_line|acceptance_test_scenario|quality_gates|implementation_notes|dependencies|estimated_hours|deliverables}

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Write failing acceptance test
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: {step-id}` (required for DES verification)
   Example:
   ```
   feat(project-id): implement feature X

   Step-ID: 02-01
   ```

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.

CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.
Orchestrator MUST NEVER write phase entries — only the agent that performed the work. A log entry without actual execution is fraud.

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.
```

**Configuration:**
- subagent_type: extracted agent name
- max_turns by step complexity:

| Step Type | Typical Tool Calls | Recommended max_turns |
|-----------|-------------------|----------------------|
| Hotfix (1 file) | 10-12 | 25 |
| Standard TDD (2-3 files) | 25-30 | 45 |
| Complex (4+ files, new module) | 35-45 | 65 |

Default: 45. Heuristic: `20 + (files_to_modify count * 8)`, capped at 65.

## Error Handling

- Invalid agent: report available agents
- Missing roadmap/execution-log: report path not found
- Step not in roadmap: report available step IDs
- Dependency failure: explain blocking tasks

## Resume vs Restart

When subagent times out:

| Last Completed Phase | Action | Rationale |
|---------------------|--------|-----------|
| GREEN (or later) | Resume | Only COMMIT remains (~5 turns) |
| RED_UNIT with partial GREEN | Resume | Preserves implementation progress |
| PREPARE or RED_ACCEPTANCE | Restart | Little context worth replaying |

Resume costs ~50% more tokens/call due to context replay (measured: 3.7K vs 2.5K tokens/call). For <5 remaining turns, resume is efficient. For 15+ turns, restart with higher max_turns is cheaper.

## Examples

```bash
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007-boundary-rules" "02-01"
/nw:execute @nw-researcher "auth-upgrade" "01-01"
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007" "03-01"  # retry after failure
```

## TDD_PHASES
<!-- Schema v4.0 — canonical source: TDDPhaseValidator.MANDATORY_PHASES -->
<!-- Build system injects mandatory phases from step-tdd-cycle-schema.json -->
{{MANDATORY_PHASES}}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Agent invoked via Task tool (dispatcher does not execute the work)
- [ ] Step context extracted from roadmap and passed in prompt
- [ ] Agent appended phase events to execution-log.yaml
- [ ] Agent did not load roadmap.yaml

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: /nw:review for post-execution review
**Deliverables**: Updated execution-log.yaml|implementation artifacts|git commits

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : policy-crud-ui -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: policy-crud-ui
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step: 01-01 - Policy route handler factory with identity guard
Description: Create policy-route.ts following learning-route.ts pattern. Wire route handler factory with identity resolution from session. Implement pure identity guard rejecting agent identities on mutation endpoints (POST, PATCH) with 403. Register routes in start-server.ts.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Agent identities receive 403 on POST /policies, PATCH /activate, PATCH /deprecate, POST /versions
- Agent identities receive 200 on GET /policies and GET /policies/:id
- Human identities pass the guard on all endpoints

Test scenarios:
- milestone-1: agent denied create (1.1)
- milestone-1: agent denied activate (1.2)
- milestone-1: agent denied deprecate (1.3)
- milestone-1: agent denied version creation (1.4)
- milestone-1: agent can read list (1.5)
- milestone-1: agent can read detail (1.6)

Files to modify/create:
- app/src/server/policy/policy-route.ts (NEW - route handler factory)
- app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts (ADD route registration)
- tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/milestone-1-authorization-and-list.test.ts (ENABLE scenarios 1.1-1.6)

Key reference files to read:
- app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts (PATTERN to follow - route handler factory)
- app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts (WHERE to register routes)
- app/src/server/policy/types.ts (existing PolicyRecord, PolicyStatus types)
- app/src/server/policy/policy-queries.ts (existing createPolicy, activatePolicy, deprecatePolicy)
- tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/policy-crud-test-kit.ts (test helpers)
- tests/acceptance/policy-crud-ui/milestone-1-authorization-and-list.test.ts (test file)
- tests/acceptance/acceptance-test-kit.ts (base test infrastructure)

Important project conventions (from AGENTS.md):
- Do NOT use null. Use undefined via optional properties (field?: Type)
- Always use --no-verify when committing, -s for GPG sign
- Functional paradigm: pure functions, pipeline composition
- Follow existing patterns in the codebase
- Use RecordId objects for Surreal identifiers, not string-based IDs
- Use record.table.name for table branching (not .tb)

Architecture notes (from design docs):
- Identity resolution follows chat-ingress.ts pattern:
  1. Better Auth session: deps.auth.api.getSession({ headers: request.headers })
  2. Person record: new RecordId("person", session.user.id)
  3. Identity lookup: SELECT VALUE in FROM identity_person WHERE out = $person LIMIT 1
  4. Identity type check: SELECT type FROM $identity
- Identity guard is a pure function: (identityType: string) => boolean
- Applied at route handler level before any mutation
- Returns 403 { error: "agents cannot modify policies" } for agent identities on mutations
- Read endpoints (GET) -> no guard, all identity types
- Mutation endpoints (POST, PATCH) -> guard required

The test file has scenarios already written but with `.skip`. You need to enable the relevant scenarios (1.1-1.6) by removing `.skip` and ensure they pass.

For this step, you need STUB implementations for the endpoints themselves (list returns empty array, detail returns 404, create/activate/deprecate/version return appropriate responses). The real implementations come in later steps. The key thing this step tests is the AUTHORIZATION GUARD - that agent identities get 403 on mutations and 200 on reads.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, read reference files, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Enable the skipped acceptance test scenarios 1.1-1.6
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test for identity guard pure function
3. GREEN - Implement policy-route.ts with guard + stub endpoints, register in start-server.ts
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: Step-ID: 01-01

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/policy-crud-ui \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/policy-crud-ui \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed above
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only via CLI)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.

Explore the policy-related code in this codebase. I need to understand:

1. Existing policy queries - find files like policy-queries.ts, policy-gate.ts, etc.
2. Existing policy types/contracts - PolicyRecord, PolicyRule, PolicyTraceEntry etc.

5. Workspace sidebar navigation - where nav items are defined
6. The existing consent page that shows intent review (where policy trace would go)
7. The schema for policy table in surreal-schema.surql

Search thoroughly across app/src/server/ and app/src/client/ directories. Return file paths and key function/type names.
